---
title: "Basic Forms — Roots, Intercepts, and Asymptotes"
format: html
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
jupyter: python3
---

For each function: **roots** (●, blue) mark where $y = 0$; **y-intercepts** (●, green) mark where $x = 0$; **asymptotes** are shown as dashed red lines.

---

## 1. Power Functions

$$y = x \qquad y = x^2 \qquad y = x^3 \qquad y = x^{-1} \qquad y = x^{1/2}$$

```{python}
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.patches as mpatches

ROOT_CLR   = "#2563EB"   # blue
YINT_CLR   = "#16A34A"   # green
ASYM_CLR   = "#DC2626"   # red
CURVE_CLR  = "#1e1e1e"

def base_ax(ax, xlim=(-3,3), ylim=(-4,4), title=""):
    ax.set_xlim(*xlim)
    ax.set_ylim(*ylim)
    ax.axhline(0, color="#888", lw=0.8)
    ax.axvline(0, color="#888", lw=0.8)
    ax.set_title(title, fontsize=11, pad=4)
    ax.tick_params(labelsize=7)
    ax.grid(True, linestyle=":", alpha=0.4)

def mark_root(ax, x, y=0):
    ax.plot(x, y, "o", color=ROOT_CLR, ms=7, zorder=5)

def mark_yint(ax, y):
    ax.plot(0, y, "s", color=YINT_CLR, ms=7, zorder=5)

def asymptote_v(ax, x):
    ax.axvline(x, color=ASYM_CLR, lw=1.2, linestyle="--", alpha=0.8)

def asymptote_h(ax, y):
    ax.axhline(y, color=ASYM_CLR, lw=1.2, linestyle="--", alpha=0.8)

# ── Power functions ───────────────────────────────────────────────
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 5, figsize=(13, 3.2))
fig.suptitle("1) Power", fontsize=12, fontweight="bold", y=1.01)

x = np.linspace(-3, 3, 400)

# y = x
ax = axes[0]; base_ax(ax, title=r"$y = x$")
ax.plot(x, x, color=CURVE_CLR, lw=2)
mark_root(ax, 0); mark_yint(ax, 0)

# y = x²
ax = axes[1]; base_ax(ax, ylim=(-1, 6), title=r"$y = x^2$")
ax.plot(x, x**2, color=CURVE_CLR, lw=2)
mark_root(ax, 0); mark_yint(ax, 0)

# y = x³
ax = axes[2]; base_ax(ax, title=r"$y = x^3$")
ax.plot(x, x**3, color=CURVE_CLR, lw=2)
mark_root(ax, 0); mark_yint(ax, 0)

# y = x⁻¹
ax = axes[3]; base_ax(ax, title=r"$y = x^{-1}$")
x_pos = np.linspace(0.1, 3, 300); x_neg = np.linspace(-3, -0.1, 300)
ax.plot(x_pos, 1/x_pos, color=CURVE_CLR, lw=2)
ax.plot(x_neg, 1/x_neg, color=CURVE_CLR, lw=2)
asymptote_v(ax, 0); asymptote_h(ax, 0)
ax.text(1.2, 3.2, "vert. asymptote\nx = 0", color=ASYM_CLR, fontsize=6.5)
ax.text(1.5, 0.4, "horiz. asymptote\ny = 0", color=ASYM_CLR, fontsize=6.5)

# y = x^(1/2)
ax = axes[4]; base_ax(ax, xlim=(-0.5, 4), ylim=(-1, 3), title=r"$y = x^{1/2}$")
x_sq = np.linspace(0, 4, 300)
ax.plot(x_sq, np.sqrt(x_sq), color=CURVE_CLR, lw=2)
mark_root(ax, 0); mark_yint(ax, 0)

legend_elements = [
    mpatches.Patch(color=ROOT_CLR, label="Root (y = 0)"),
    mpatches.Patch(color=YINT_CLR, label="y-intercept (x = 0)"),
    mpatches.Patch(color=ASYM_CLR, label="Asymptote"),
]
fig.legend(handles=legend_elements, loc="lower center", ncol=3,
           fontsize=8, bbox_to_anchor=(0.5, -0.12), framealpha=0.9)

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

| Function | Roots | y-intercept | Asymptotes |
|----------|-------|-------------|------------|
| $y = x$ | $(0,0)$ | $(0,0)$ | none |
| $y = x^2$ | $(0,0)$ | $(0,0)$ | none |
| $y = x^3$ | $(0,0)$ | $(0,0)$ | none |
| $y = x^{-1}$ | none | none | vert. $x=0$, horiz. $y=0$ |
| $y = x^{1/2}$ | $(0,0)$ | $(0,0)$ | none (domain $x \ge 0$) |

---

## 2. Exponential / Logarithmic

$$y = e^x \qquad\qquad y = \ln x$$

```{python}
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(7, 3.2))
fig.suptitle("2) Exponential / Logarithmic", fontsize=12, fontweight="bold", y=1.01)

# y = eˣ
ax = axes[0]; base_ax(ax, xlim=(-3,3), ylim=(-1, 7), title=r"$y = e^x$")
x = np.linspace(-3, 2, 400)
ax.plot(x, np.exp(x), color=CURVE_CLR, lw=2)
asymptote_h(ax, 0)
mark_yint(ax, 1)            # y-intercept at (0, 1)
ax.text(0.1, 0.35, "y-int (0,1)", color=YINT_CLR, fontsize=7)
ax.text(-2.8, 0.35, "horiz. asymptote y = 0", color=ASYM_CLR, fontsize=6.5)

# y = ln x
ax = axes[1]; base_ax(ax, xlim=(-0.5, 5), ylim=(-4, 3), title=r"$y = \ln x$")
x_ln = np.linspace(0.02, 5, 400)
ax.plot(x_ln, np.log(x_ln), color=CURVE_CLR, lw=2)
asymptote_v(ax, 0)
mark_root(ax, 1, 0)          # root at (1, 0)
ax.text(1.1, 0.15, "root (1,0)", color=ROOT_CLR, fontsize=7)
ax.text(0.15, -3, "vert. asymptote\nx = 0", color=ASYM_CLR, fontsize=6.5)

fig.legend(handles=legend_elements, loc="lower center", ncol=3,
           fontsize=8, bbox_to_anchor=(0.5, -0.12), framealpha=0.9)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

| Function | Roots | y-intercept | Asymptotes |
|----------|-------|-------------|------------|
| $y = e^x$ | none | $(0,1)$ | horiz. $y=0$ (as $x\to-\infty$) |
| $y = \ln x$ | $(1,0)$ | none | vert. $x=0$ (domain $x>0$) |

---

## 3. Trigonometric

### 3a. sin, cos, tan

```{python}
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 3, figsize=(13, 3.5))
fig.suptitle("3a) Trigonometric — sin, cos, tan", fontsize=12, fontweight="bold", y=1.01)

T = 2*np.pi
x = np.linspace(-T, T, 1000)

# y = sin x
ax = axes[0]; base_ax(ax, xlim=(-T, T), ylim=(-2,2), title=r"$y = \sin x$")
ax.plot(x, np.sin(x), color=CURVE_CLR, lw=2)
for r in [-2*np.pi, -np.pi, 0, np.pi, 2*np.pi]:
    mark_root(ax, r)
mark_yint(ax, 0)
ax.set_xticks([-2*np.pi,-np.pi,0,np.pi,2*np.pi])
ax.set_xticklabels([r"$-2\pi$",r"$-\pi$","$0$",r"$\pi$",r"$2\pi$"], fontsize=7)

# y = cos x
ax = axes[1]; base_ax(ax, xlim=(-T, T), ylim=(-2,2), title=r"$y = \cos x$")
ax.plot(x, np.cos(x), color=CURVE_CLR, lw=2)
for r in [-3*np.pi/2, -np.pi/2, np.pi/2, 3*np.pi/2]:
    mark_root(ax, r)
mark_yint(ax, 1)
ax.set_xticks([-2*np.pi,-np.pi,0,np.pi,2*np.pi])
ax.set_xticklabels([r"$-2\pi$",r"$-\pi$","$0$",r"$\pi$",r"$2\pi$"], fontsize=7)

# y = tan x
ax = axes[2]; base_ax(ax, xlim=(-T, T), ylim=(-4,4), title=r"$y = \tan x$")
for k in [-1, 0, 1]:
    x_seg = np.linspace(k*np.pi - np.pi/2 + 0.05, k*np.pi + np.pi/2 - 0.05, 300)
    ax.plot(x_seg, np.tan(x_seg), color=CURVE_CLR, lw=2)
for a in [-3*np.pi/2, -np.pi/2, np.pi/2, 3*np.pi/2]:
    asymptote_v(ax, a)
for r in [-np.pi, 0, np.pi]:
    mark_root(ax, r)
mark_yint(ax, 0)
ax.set_xticks([-2*np.pi,-np.pi,0,np.pi,2*np.pi])
ax.set_xticklabels([r"$-2\pi$",r"$-\pi$","$0$",r"$\pi$",r"$2\pi$"], fontsize=7)

fig.legend(handles=legend_elements, loc="lower center", ncol=3,
           fontsize=8, bbox_to_anchor=(0.5, -0.12), framealpha=0.9)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

| Function | Roots | y-intercept | Asymptotes |
|----------|-------|-------------|------------|
| $y = \sin x$ | $x = n\pi$ | $(0,0)$ | none |
| $y = \cos x$ | $x = \tfrac{\pi}{2}+n\pi$ | $(0,1)$ | none |
| $y = \tan x$ | $x = n\pi$ | $(0,0)$ | vert. $x = \tfrac{\pi}{2}+n\pi$ |

### 3b. csc, sec, cot

```{python}
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 3, figsize=(13, 3.5))
fig.suptitle("3b) Trigonometric — csc, sec, cot", fontsize=12, fontweight="bold", y=1.01)

eps = 0.08

# y = csc x
ax = axes[0]; base_ax(ax, xlim=(-T, T), ylim=(-5,5), title=r"$y = \csc x$")
for k in [-1, 0, 1]:
    seg = np.linspace(k*np.pi + eps, (k+1)*np.pi - eps, 300)
    ax.plot(seg, 1/np.sin(seg), color=CURVE_CLR, lw=2)
for a in [-2*np.pi, -np.pi, 0, np.pi, 2*np.pi]:
    asymptote_v(ax, a)
ax.set_xticks([-2*np.pi,-np.pi,0,np.pi,2*np.pi])
ax.set_xticklabels([r"$-2\pi$",r"$-\pi$","$0$",r"$\pi$",r"$2\pi$"], fontsize=7)

# y = sec x
ax = axes[1]; base_ax(ax, xlim=(-T, T), ylim=(-5,5), title=r"$y = \sec x$")
for k in [-2, -1, 0, 1]:
    seg = np.linspace(k*np.pi - np.pi/2 + eps, k*np.pi + np.pi/2 - eps, 300)
    ax.plot(seg, 1/np.cos(seg), color=CURVE_CLR, lw=2)
for a in [-3*np.pi/2, -np.pi/2, np.pi/2, 3*np.pi/2]:
    asymptote_v(ax, a)
mark_yint(ax, 1)
ax.set_xticks([-2*np.pi,-np.pi,0,np.pi,2*np.pi])
ax.set_xticklabels([r"$-2\pi$",r"$-\pi$","$0$",r"$\pi$",r"$2\pi$"], fontsize=7)

# y = cot x
ax = axes[2]; base_ax(ax, xlim=(-T, T), ylim=(-5,5), title=r"$y = \cot x$")
for k in [-1, 0, 1]:
    seg = np.linspace(k*np.pi + eps, (k+1)*np.pi - eps, 300)
    ax.plot(seg, 1/np.tan(seg), color=CURVE_CLR, lw=2)
for a in [-2*np.pi, -np.pi, 0, np.pi, 2*np.pi]:
    asymptote_v(ax, a)
for r in [-3*np.pi/2, -np.pi/2, np.pi/2, 3*np.pi/2]:
    mark_root(ax, r)
ax.set_xticks([-2*np.pi,-np.pi,0,np.pi,2*np.pi])
ax.set_xticklabels([r"$-2\pi$",r"$-\pi$","$0$",r"$\pi$",r"$2\pi$"], fontsize=7)

fig.legend(handles=legend_elements, loc="lower center", ncol=3,
           fontsize=8, bbox_to_anchor=(0.5, -0.12), framealpha=0.9)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

| Function | Roots | y-intercept | Asymptotes |
|----------|-------|-------------|------------|
| $y = \csc x$ | none | none | vert. $x = n\pi$ |
| $y = \sec x$ | none | $(0,1)$ | vert. $x = \tfrac{\pi}{2}+n\pi$ |
| $y = \cot x$ | $x = \tfrac{\pi}{2}+n\pi$ | none | vert. $x = n\pi$ |

### 3c. arcsin, arccos, arctan

```{python}
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 3, figsize=(13, 3.5))
fig.suptitle("3c) Trigonometric — arcsin, arccos, arctan", fontsize=12, fontweight="bold", y=1.01)

# y = arcsin x  (domain [-1,1], range [-π/2, π/2])
ax = axes[0]; base_ax(ax, xlim=(-1.5, 1.5), ylim=(-2, 2), title=r"$y = \arcsin x$")
x_arc = np.linspace(-1, 1, 300)
ax.plot(x_arc, np.arcsin(x_arc), color=CURVE_CLR, lw=2)
mark_root(ax, 0); mark_yint(ax, 0)
ax.set_xticks([-1, 0, 1]); ax.set_yticks([-np.pi/2, 0, np.pi/2])
ax.set_yticklabels([r"$-\pi/2$", "$0$", r"$\pi/2$"], fontsize=7)

# y = arccos x  (domain [-1,1], range [0, π])
ax = axes[1]; base_ax(ax, xlim=(-1.5, 1.5), ylim=(-0.5, 4), title=r"$y = \arccos x$")
ax.plot(x_arc, np.arccos(x_arc), color=CURVE_CLR, lw=2)
mark_root(ax, 1, 0)          # arccos(1) = 0
mark_yint(ax, np.pi/2)       # arccos(0) = π/2
ax.text(0.05, np.pi/2 + 0.15, r"y-int $(0,\pi/2)$", color=YINT_CLR, fontsize=6.5)
ax.text(1.05, 0.15, "root (1,0)", color=ROOT_CLR, fontsize=6.5)
ax.set_xticks([-1, 0, 1]); ax.set_yticks([0, np.pi/2, np.pi])
ax.set_yticklabels(["$0$", r"$\pi/2$", r"$\pi$"], fontsize=7)

# y = arctan x  (range (-π/2, π/2))
ax = axes[2]; base_ax(ax, xlim=(-5, 5), ylim=(-2, 2), title=r"$y = \arctan x$")
x_at = np.linspace(-5, 5, 400)
ax.plot(x_at, np.arctan(x_at), color=CURVE_CLR, lw=2)
asymptote_h(ax,  np.pi/2)
asymptote_h(ax, -np.pi/2)
mark_root(ax, 0); mark_yint(ax, 0)
ax.set_yticks([-np.pi/2, 0, np.pi/2])
ax.set_yticklabels([r"$-\pi/2$", "$0$", r"$\pi/2$"], fontsize=7)
ax.text(1.5,  np.pi/2 + 0.15, r"horiz. asymptote $y=\pi/2$", color=ASYM_CLR, fontsize=6)
ax.text(1.5, -np.pi/2 - 0.35, r"horiz. asymptote $y=-\pi/2$", color=ASYM_CLR, fontsize=6)

fig.legend(handles=legend_elements, loc="lower center", ncol=3,
           fontsize=8, bbox_to_anchor=(0.5, -0.12), framealpha=0.9)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

| Function | Roots | y-intercept | Asymptotes |
|----------|-------|-------------|------------|
| $y = \arcsin x$ | $(0,0)$ | $(0,0)$ | none (domain $[-1,1]$) |
| $y = \arccos x$ | $(1,0)$ | $(0,\pi/2)$ | none (domain $[-1,1]$) |
| $y = \arctan x$ | $(0,0)$ | $(0,0)$ | horiz. $y = \pm\pi/2$ |

---

## 2. Single Transformation

Each transformation comes in two flavours — **vertical** (↕) and **horizontal** (↔).

| Type | Vertical ↕ | Horizontal ↔ |
|------|-----------|--------------|
| **Translation** | $f(x) \to f(x)+a$ | $f(x) \to f(x+a)$ |
| **Stretch** | $f(x) \to af(x)$ | $f(x) \to f(ax)$ |
| **Reflection** | $f(x) \to -f(x)$ | $f(x) \to f(-x)$ |
| **Modulus** | $f(x) \to \lvert f(x)\rvert$ | $f(x) \to f(\lvert x\rvert)$ |

::: {.callout-tip title="KEY — Modulus as conditional reflection"}
$$
\lvert f(x)\rvert = \begin{cases} f(x), & \text{if } f(x)\ge 0 \\ -f(x), & \text{if } f(x)<0 \end{cases}
\qquad\text{(reflect the part below the }x\text{-axis upward)}
$$
$$
f(\lvert x\rvert) = \begin{cases} f(x), & \text{if } x\ge 0 \\ f(-x), & \text{if } x<0 \end{cases}
\qquad\text{(reflect the right half onto the left half)}
$$
:::

---

### Example 1 — Modulus sketch and intersection

**Given** $f(x)=|x|+2$ and $g(x)=3$:

i. Sketch $f(x)$ and $g(x)$ on the same diagram.
ii. How many intersection points are there?
iii. Hence, solve $f(x)=g(x)$.

```{python}
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.patches as mpatches

ROOT_CLR  = "#2563EB"
YINT_CLR  = "#16A34A"
ASYM_CLR  = "#DC2626"
CURVE_CLR = "#1e1e1e"
INTER_CLR = "#9333EA"   # purple — intersection points

def base_ax(ax, xlim=(-4,4), ylim=(-1,6), title=""):
    ax.set_xlim(*xlim); ax.set_ylim(*ylim)
    ax.axhline(0, color="#888", lw=0.8)
    ax.axvline(0, color="#888", lw=0.8)
    ax.set_title(title, fontsize=11, pad=4)
    ax.tick_params(labelsize=8)
    ax.grid(True, linestyle=":", alpha=0.4)

def mark_root(ax, x, y=0, **kw):  ax.plot(x, y, "o", color=ROOT_CLR, ms=7, zorder=5, **kw)
def mark_yint(ax, y, **kw):        ax.plot(0, y, "s", color=YINT_CLR, ms=7, zorder=5, **kw)
def asymptote_v(ax, x):            ax.axvline(x, color=ASYM_CLR, lw=1.2, ls="--", alpha=0.8)
def asymptote_h(ax, y):            ax.axhline(y, color=ASYM_CLR, lw=1.2, ls="--", alpha=0.8)
def mark_inter(ax, x, y, **kw):   ax.plot(x, y, "*", color=INTER_CLR, ms=11, zorder=6, **kw)

legend_elements = [
    mpatches.Patch(color=ROOT_CLR,  label="Root  (y = 0)"),
    mpatches.Patch(color=YINT_CLR,  label="y-intercept  (x = 0)"),
    mpatches.Patch(color=ASYM_CLR,  label="Asymptote"),
    mpatches.Patch(color=INTER_CLR, label="Intersection"),
]

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(5, 4))
base_ax(ax, xlim=(-4,4), ylim=(-0.5, 5), title=r"$f(x)=|x|+2$ and $g(x)=3$")

x = np.linspace(-4, 4, 400)
ax.plot(x, np.abs(x)+2, color=CURVE_CLR, lw=2, label=r"$f(x)=|x|+2$")
ax.plot(x, np.full_like(x, 3), color="#0891B2", lw=2, ls="-", label=r"$g(x)=3$")

# key points
mark_yint(ax, 2)            # f: y-intercept (0, 2)
ax.annotate("y-int (0,2)", (0,2), (-1.5, 1.5), fontsize=7, color=YINT_CLR,
            arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle="->", color=YINT_CLR, lw=0.8))

# f has no real roots (min value = 2)
ax.text(-3.8, 2.15, "no roots\n(min = 2)", fontsize=7, color=ROOT_CLR)

# intersections at x = ±1
for xi, yi in [(-1, 3), (1, 3)]:
    mark_inter(ax, xi, yi)
    ax.annotate(f"({xi},{yi})", (xi, yi), (xi-0.6, yi+0.35), fontsize=7, color=INTER_CLR,
                arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle="->", color=INTER_CLR, lw=0.8))

ax.legend(fontsize=8, loc="upper right")
fig.legend(handles=legend_elements, loc="lower center", ncol=2,
           fontsize=7.5, bbox_to_anchor=(0.5, -0.08), framealpha=0.9)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

**Solution:**

$$
|x|+2 = 3 \implies |x|=1 \implies x = \pm 1
$$

There are **2 intersection points**: $(-1,\,3)$ and $(1,\,3)$.

---

### Example 2 — Two modulus functions

**Given** $f(x)=|x|$ and $g(x)=|x+1|$:

i. Sketch $f(x)$ and $g(x)$ on the same diagram.
ii. Hence, solve $f(x)=g(x)$.

```{python}
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(5, 4))
base_ax(ax, xlim=(-3.5, 3.5), ylim=(-0.5, 4),
        title=r"$f(x)=|x|$ and $g(x)=|x+1|$")

x = np.linspace(-3.5, 3.5, 400)
ax.plot(x, np.abs(x),   color=CURVE_CLR, lw=2, label=r"$f(x)=|x|$")
ax.plot(x, np.abs(x+1), color="#0891B2", lw=2, label=r"$g(x)=|x+1|$")

# f(x)=|x|: root and y-intercept both at (0,0)
mark_root(ax, 0, label="_nolegend_")
mark_yint(ax, 0)
ax.annotate("root & y-int\n(0, 0)  [f]", (0.05, 0.05), (0.6, 0.7),
            fontsize=7, color=YINT_CLR,
            arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle="->", color=YINT_CLR, lw=0.8))

# g(x)=|x+1|: root at (-1,0), y-intercept at (0,1)
mark_root(ax, -1, 0)
ax.annotate("root (-1,0)  [g]", (-1, 0), (-2.5, 0.5), fontsize=7, color=ROOT_CLR,
            arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle="->", color=ROOT_CLR, lw=0.8))
mark_yint(ax, 1)
ax.annotate("y-int (0,1)  [g]", (0, 1), (0.6, 1.5), fontsize=7, color=YINT_CLR,
            arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle="->", color=YINT_CLR, lw=0.8))

# intersection at x = -1/2, y = 1/2
mark_inter(ax, -0.5, 0.5)
ax.annotate(r"$(-\frac{1}{2},\,\frac{1}{2})$", (-0.5, 0.5), (-2, 1.5), fontsize=8,
            color=INTER_CLR,
            arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle="->", color=INTER_CLR, lw=0.8))

ax.legend(fontsize=8, loc="upper right")
fig.legend(handles=legend_elements, loc="lower center", ncol=2,
           fontsize=7.5, bbox_to_anchor=(0.5, -0.08), framealpha=0.9)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

**Solution:**

$$
|x| = |x+1| \implies x^2 = (x+1)^2 \implies 2x+1=0 \implies x = -\tfrac{1}{2}
$$

One intersection point: $\left(-\tfrac{1}{2},\,\tfrac{1}{2}\right)$.

---

## 3. Combination of Transformations

::: {.callout-important title="KEY — Order of operations"}
Apply transformations in this order:

$$\text{stretch / reflection} \;(\times) \;\longrightarrow\; \text{translation} \;(+) \;\longrightarrow\; \text{modulus}$$
:::

---

### Example 3 — Describe in words

Describe each combination of transformations applied to a base function.

| | Function | Base | Transformations (in order) |
|-|----------|------|---------------------------|
| i | $y = 2x+3$ | $y=x$ | stretch vert. ×2, then translate up 3 |
| ii | $y = -1+e^{3x}$ | $y=e^x$ | stretch horiz. ×$\tfrac{1}{3}$, then translate down 1 |
| iii | $y = -1+2e^{3x}$ | $y=e^x$ | stretch horiz. ×$\tfrac{1}{3}$, stretch vert. ×2, then translate down 1 |
| iv | $y = 5-2e^{-3x}$ | $y=e^x$ | reflect horiz. ($x\to -x$), stretch horiz. ×$\tfrac{1}{3}$, stretch vert. ×2, reflect vert., then translate up 5 |

---

### Example 4 — Sketch combinations (with all key features)

**i.** $y = -1 + \sqrt{x-3}$

```{python}
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(5,4))
base_ax(ax, xlim=(-0.5, 9), ylim=(-2.5, 3),
        title=r"$y = -1 + \sqrt{x-3}$")

x_sq = np.linspace(3, 9, 300)
ax.plot(x_sq, -1 + np.sqrt(x_sq - 3), color=CURVE_CLR, lw=2)

# Start point / domain boundary
ax.plot(3, -1, "D", color="#F59E0B", ms=8, zorder=5, label="Domain start")
ax.annotate("domain start\n(3, −1)", (3,-1), (4.2,-1.8), fontsize=7, color="#F59E0B",
            arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle="->", color="#F59E0B", lw=0.8))

# Root: -1+sqrt(x-3)=0 → x-3=1 → x=4
mark_root(ax, 4, 0)
ax.annotate("root (4, 0)", (4,0), (5.5, 0.4), fontsize=7, color=ROOT_CLR,
            arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle="->", color=ROOT_CLR, lw=0.8))

# No y-intercept (domain x≥3)
ax.text(0.1, -2.2, "no y-intercept\n(domain: $x\\geq 3$)", fontsize=7, color="#6B7280")

ax.legend(fontsize=8)
fig.legend(handles=legend_elements, loc="lower center", ncol=2,
           fontsize=7.5, bbox_to_anchor=(0.5, -0.08), framealpha=0.9)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

| Feature | Value |
|---------|-------|
| Root | $(4, 0)$ |
| y-intercept | none (domain $x \ge 3$) |
| Asymptote | none |
| Base | $y=\sqrt{x}$, translated right 3, down 1 |

---

**ii.** $y = 1 + \dfrac{3}{x-2}$ — *always transform the asymptote first!*

```{python}
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(5,4))
base_ax(ax, xlim=(-4, 7), ylim=(-5, 7),
        title=r"$y = 1 + \dfrac{3}{x-2}$")

x_pos = np.linspace(2.05, 7,  300)
x_neg = np.linspace(-4,  1.95, 300)
ax.plot(x_pos, 1 + 3/(x_pos-2), color=CURVE_CLR, lw=2)
ax.plot(x_neg, 1 + 3/(x_neg-2), color=CURVE_CLR, lw=2)

asymptote_v(ax, 2)
asymptote_h(ax, 1)
ax.text(2.1, 6.2, "vert. asymptote\n$x=2$", fontsize=7, color=ASYM_CLR)
ax.text(-3.9, 1.2, "horiz. asymptote  $y=1$", fontsize=7, color=ASYM_CLR)

# Root: 1+3/(x-2)=0 → x-2=-3 → x=-1
mark_root(ax, -1, 0)
ax.annotate("root (−1, 0)", (-1,0), (-3,-1.2), fontsize=7, color=ROOT_CLR,
            arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle="->", color=ROOT_CLR, lw=0.8))

# y-intercept: x=0 → 1+3/(0-2)=1-3/2=-1/2
mark_yint(ax, -0.5)
ax.annotate(r"y-int $(0,\,-\frac{1}{2})$", (0,-0.5), (1.2,-2.5), fontsize=7, color=YINT_CLR,
            arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle="->", color=YINT_CLR, lw=0.8))

fig.legend(handles=legend_elements, loc="lower center", ncol=2,
           fontsize=7.5, bbox_to_anchor=(0.5, -0.08), framealpha=0.9)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

| Feature | Value |
|---------|-------|
| Root | $(-1, 0)$ |
| y-intercept | $\left(0,\,-\tfrac{1}{2}\right)$ |
| Vert. asymptote | $x = 2$ |
| Horiz. asymptote | $y = 1$ |

---

**iii.** $y = \left\lvert\dfrac{x+1}{x-2}\right\rvert$

```{python}
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(5,4))
base_ax(ax, xlim=(-5, 7), ylim=(-0.3, 4),
        title=r"$y = \left|\dfrac{x+1}{x-2}\right|$")

x_left  = np.linspace(-5, 1.92, 400)
x_right = np.linspace(2.08, 7,  400)
ax.plot(x_left,  np.abs((x_left+1) /(x_left-2)),  color=CURVE_CLR, lw=2)
ax.plot(x_right, np.abs((x_right+1)/(x_right-2)), color=CURVE_CLR, lw=2)

asymptote_v(ax, 2)
asymptote_h(ax, 1)
ax.text(2.1, 3.7, "vert. asymptote\n$x=2$", fontsize=7, color=ASYM_CLR)
ax.text(-4.8, 1.1, "horiz. asymptote  $y=1$", fontsize=7, color=ASYM_CLR)

# Root at x=-1 (numerator=0, denominator≠0)
mark_root(ax, -1, 0)
ax.annotate("root (−1, 0)", (-1,0), (-3.5, 0.6), fontsize=7, color=ROOT_CLR,
            arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle="->", color=ROOT_CLR, lw=0.8))

# y-intercept: |(0+1)/(0-2)| = 1/2
mark_yint(ax, 0.5)
ax.annotate(r"y-int $(0,\,\frac{1}{2})$", (0,0.5), (1.2, 0.2), fontsize=7, color=YINT_CLR,
            arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle="->", color=YINT_CLR, lw=0.8))

fig.legend(handles=legend_elements, loc="lower center", ncol=2,
           fontsize=7.5, bbox_to_anchor=(0.5, -0.08), framealpha=0.9)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

| Feature | Value |
|---------|-------|
| Root | $(-1, 0)$ |
| y-intercept | $\left(0,\,\tfrac{1}{2}\right)$ |
| Vert. asymptote | $x = 2$ |
| Horiz. asymptote | $y = 1$ (both branches approach from above) |

---

**iv.** $y = 2\csc\!\left(x+\dfrac{\pi}{3}\right)$, $\quad 0 < x < 2\pi$

```{python}
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(6,4))
base_ax(ax, xlim=(0, 2*np.pi), ylim=(-6, 6),
        title=r"$y = 2\csc\!\left(x+\frac{\pi}{3}\right),\quad 0 < x < 2\pi$")

eps = 0.06
# sin(x+π/3)=0 ⟹ x = -π/3, 2π/3, 5π/3  (only 2π/3 and 5π/3 inside (0,2π))
a1 = 2*np.pi/3
a2 = 5*np.pi/3

segs = [
    (0 + eps,   a1 - eps),
    (a1 + eps,  a2 - eps),
    (a2 + eps,  2*np.pi - eps),
]
for lo, hi in segs:
    xs = np.linspace(lo, hi, 400)
    ax.plot(xs, 2/np.sin(xs + np.pi/3), color=CURVE_CLR, lw=2)

asymptote_v(ax, a1)
asymptote_v(ax, a2)
ax.text(a1 + 0.05, 5.3, r"$x=\frac{2\pi}{3}$", fontsize=8, color=ASYM_CLR)
ax.text(a2 + 0.05, 5.3, r"$x=\frac{5\pi}{3}$", fontsize=8, color=ASYM_CLR)

# No roots (2/sin(...) is never 0)
ax.text(0.1, -5.5, "no roots  (2/sin never = 0)", fontsize=7, color="#6B7280")

# No y-intercept (open domain 0 < x < 2π, x=0 excluded)
ax.text(0.1, 5.5, r"no y-intercept  ($x=0$ excluded)", fontsize=7, color="#6B7280")

ax.set_xticks([0, np.pi/2, np.pi, 3*np.pi/2, 2*np.pi])
ax.set_xticklabels(["$0$", r"$\pi/2$", r"$\pi$", r"$3\pi/2$", r"$2\pi$"], fontsize=8)

fig.legend(handles=legend_elements, loc="lower center", ncol=2,
           fontsize=7.5, bbox_to_anchor=(0.5, -0.08), framealpha=0.9)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

| Feature | Value |
|---------|-------|
| Roots | none |
| y-intercept | none (open domain $0 < x < 2\pi$) |
| Vert. asymptotes | $x = \tfrac{2\pi}{3}$, $x = \tfrac{5\pi}{3}$ |
| Amplitude | $\times 2$ stretch of $\csc x$; horizontal shift $-\tfrac{\pi}{3}$ |

> **Note:** $\sin(x+\frac{\pi}{3})=0$ gives $x+\frac{\pi}{3}=n\pi$, so $x = n\pi - \frac{\pi}{3}$.
> Within $(0, 2\pi)$: $x = \frac{2\pi}{3}$ (when $n=1$) and $x = \frac{5\pi}{3}$ (when $n=2$).

---

## Quick Reference

| Category | Horiz. asymptote | Vert. asymptote | Repeating roots |
|----------|-----------------|-----------------|----------------|
| $x^{-1}$ | $y=0$ | $x=0$ | — |
| $e^x$ | $y=0$ | — | — |
| $\ln x$ | — | $x=0$ | — |
| $\tan x, \cot x$ | — | every $\tfrac{\pi}{2}$ | every $\pi$ |
| $\csc x, \sec x$ | — | every $\pi$ (shifted) | none |
| $\arctan x$ | $y=\pm\tfrac{\pi}{2}$ | — | — |
